next_priority: All 7 targets at phase 2. Task 3 (Lean formal spec) blocked by Lean toolchain in sandbox. Priority order: ArgumentArity > TryUnescape > TreeNodeFilter > SplitCommandLine > ParseOptionAndSeparators > CommandLineOptionsValidator > CommandLineParseResult.Equals.

findings.desc: BUG-1: Single-char quote input -> IndexOf(char, 1, -1) throws ArgumentOutOfRangeException.
BUG-2: Single-char double-quote input -> input[1..^1] range exception.
Unclosed opening quote at start silently discards all input content.
Adjacent quoted strings emit 2 tokens not 1. Closing quote triggers yield+reset.
Empty option name not rejected: '--:value' produces currentOption='' silently.
OQ-3: KeyNotFoundException if unknown option bypasses ValidateNoUnknownOptions step.
EC5: Absent required options (Min>0 but option not in parseResult) are not caught by arity check; delegated to ValidateConfigurationAsync.

lean_setup.note: lakefile.toml still has mathlib — the PR to remove it was closed without merge. Remove it manually when writing the first .lean file.
lean_setup.elan_install_note: Network blocked in sandbox. curl is blocked entirely. Use leanprover/lean4-action in CI. No local lean install possible in this environment.
lean_setup.important_note: String.replace, String.startsWith etc are @[extern] and NOT kernel-reducible. Use native_decide for concrete string evaluation. decide works for pure Prop/Bool/Int without String ops. omega for integer arithmetic.
lean_setup.argument_arity_lean_notes: ArgumentArity.lean was written with 27 theorems, 0 sorry. The PR was closed without merge. When Lean is available, re-create the file — it proved well.
